title: gentle pillowy tunisian oat tagine
description: a soothing and comforting tunisian-inspired dish featuring oat milk and tempering chocolate, perfect for a sick day. the pillowy texture and herbal flavors will help ease your symptoms.
instructions: in a raclette grill or heavy-bottomed pot, toast the oats over low heat, stirring frequently, until lightly browned and fragrant.
in a blender or food processor, combine the oat milk, tempering chocolate, ginger, lemon juice, honey, cinnamon, and cayenne pepper (if using).
transfer the blended mixture to the raclette grill or pot with the toasted oats.
cook over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the mixture has thickened and the oats are fully incorporated, about 10 minutes.
remove from heat and let cool slightly.
serve warm, garnished with a sprinkle of cinnamon or a drizzle of honey, if desired.
note: if using a raclette grill, you can also cook the tagine in individual portions by pouring the mixture into the grill's wells and cooking for 2-3 minutes on each side.
